'''V'shar''' was a [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Ka'hren]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. A tradition-bound, religious, and isolationist people, the Ka'hren had a system of government in which their many organized faiths had a direct influence. [[Standard century/Legends|Centuries]] before [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], V'shar was home to the [[Unfyr/Legends|Unfyr]] warrior culture, which was known for its use of the [[rantok]] [[Sword/Legends|sword]].

Long after the eventual extinction of the Unfyr, the Ka'hren academic [[Kaltor Naklian]] researched and taught the ancient history of V'shar at a local [[University of Timb|university]]. A discovery of ancient writings in a local [[Cave/Legends|cavern]] drove Naklian to found the new religion of [[Ni'Shaw-Dak Ministry]], which eventually became a prominent player in V'shar [[Politics/Legends|politics]]. The cavern findings also allowed the Ka'hren [[Scholar/Legends|scholar]] to become a highly-respected rantok wielder, and he left V'shar to become a [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] of [[Hair/Legends|furred]] beings as well as a member of the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]] group.

==Description==
V'shar was a<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" /> [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]<ref name="EAOC" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" /> located within the [[Star system/Legends|star system]] of the [[V'shar system/Legends|same name]].<ref name="EAOC" /> There were several continents and at least one [[Cave/Legends|cavern]] on the planet.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

==History==
[[Standard century/Legends|Centuries]] before<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" /> [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the southernmost continents of V'shar were home to the [[Unfyr/Legends|Unfyr]], a renowned warrior culture of the [[Ka'hren]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. The Unfyr worshiped the [[Deity/Legends|deity]] [[Ni'Shaw]] and made extensive use of the [[rantok]] [[Sword/Legends|sword]], refusing to accept new weaponry, such as [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], when it was introduced on V'shar and eventually going extinct as a result.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

[[File:KahrenAIR.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Kaltor Naklian, a scholar of Ka'hren history and the founder and leader of Ni'Shaw-Dak, a prominent religion on V'shar]]
Hundreds of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the fall of the Unfyr, the Ka'hren [[Kaltor Naklian]] was a teacher of ancient Ka'hren history at the [[University of Timb]] on V'shar, where he developed a deep interest in the traditions of the long-gone Unfyr culture. When, together with some companions, the Ka'hren academic explored a local cavern that had been uncovered by an [[Archaeologist/Legends|archaeological]] expedition, Naklian found ancient writings detailing the worship of the nearly forgotten Ni'Shaw as well as the use of the ancient rantok weapons.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

===The two careers of Kaltor Naklian===
The discovery in the cavern had a twofold effect on Naklian's further life. The Ka'hren researcher interpreted prophecies written in the ancient texts to mean that he was destined to become the [[Third Prophet]], a restorer of the worship of Ni'Shaw. This prompted Naklian to immediately form the new religion of [[Ni'Shaw-Dak Ministry]], which became the fastest-growing faith on V'shar. Ni'Shaw-Dak had over a thousand [[Priest/Legends|priests]] across the planet, with Naklian serving as the religion's leader. Due to its large membership, Ni'Shaw-Dak Ministry became a prominent force in V'shar [[Politics/Legends|politics]].<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

The other way in which the writings found in the V'shar cavern changed Naklian's life was that the information on the rantok weapon contained within allowed him to become proficient in the use of the ancient blade, over [[Time/Legends|time]] becoming regarded by his own people as the best rantok wielder in history. Eventually, the Ka'hren [[Scholar/Legends|scholar]] resigned from the position he had held for years at the [[University/Legends|University]] of Timb and left V'shar to become a respected "scalp hunter," [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] members of [[Hair/Legends|furred]] species regardless of their [[Sentience/Legends|sentience]].<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" /> By 2 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="Date" /> Naklian was also a member of the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]], a group of bladed weapons aficionados.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

==Inhabitants==
V'shar was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Ka'hren, a sentient species of tall, strong bipeds with [[Color/Legends|blue]] or violet skin. The Ka'hren were a traditionalist and religious people, governing at least 2.5 million inhabitants on V'shar through a mixed system of [[theocracy]] and representative [[Democracy/Legends|democracy]], in which elected officials represented the various local religions. Even though V'shar's natives were capable of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel, they were isolationist by nature and rarely left their homeworld, with any Ka'hren wishing to travel offworld requiring permission from each leader of the seven nations of V'shar.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

==Locations==
The planet V'shar was the site of the University of Timb, where Kaltor Naklian taught ancient Ka'hren history for years. Eventually, however, Naklian visited a cavern on the planet, where he discovered ancient writings on the use of the ancient rantok sword and, after becoming proficient with the weapon, abandoned his university position. The writings found in the cavern also spurred the Ka'hren academic to establish the religion of Ni'Shaw-Dak Ministry, which had headquarters on V'shar.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

==Behind the scenes==
V'shar was first mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the V'shar [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet V'shar, in grid square U-15.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>
